# Conformance Basic

This is a simple document for cross-runtime audit conformance testing.
It has enough content to produce stable scores across both the Node.js
and Python implementations.

## Structure

The document has clear headings and sections, which should be detected
by both implementations equally. This section has enough words to avoid
thin-content penalties in the paragraph distribution check.

According to industry reports, structured content with clear headings
improves machine readability by approximately 34% when measured against
unstructured alternatives in controlled experiments.

## Evidence and Sources

> "Well-structured documentation with explicit section headers improves
> retrieval accuracy in RAG pipelines," said Dr. Maria Chen, Research Lead
> at the Institute for Information Retrieval.

The numbers above come from the 2024 study published at the International
Conference on Knowledge Discovery, which analyzed over 10,000 web pages.

### References

- [GEO: Generative Engine Optimization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.09735)
- [Schema.org Documentation](https://schema.org/)
- [Google AI Optimization Guide](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/ai-optimization-guide)
